hero_025.earlyGame: In the early game, use Skill 2 to shoot a fire arrow, creating a combustion area lasting 6.5 seconds to harass enemies with continuous magical damage. The Passive allows normal attacks and skills to apply Ignite marks to enemies; at 4 stacks, they are ignited, dealing additional magical damage and slowing their movement speed, giving you the upper hand.
hero_025.midGame: Utilize the strong wind from Skill 1 to expand the combustion area created by Skill 2 in a designated direction, dealing continuous magical damage to enemies in a wide range. It increases allies' movement speed while reducing enemies' movement speed, and the Passive's healing effect (restoring HP when you are inside a combustion area) enhances survivability.
hero_025.lateGame: Create up to 6 combustion areas and cast Skill 3's flame to pass through them, generating a powerful hot wind. This deals massive damage to enemies and applies a 1-second stun. Combined with the Passive's ignite damage, it allows you to control teamfights.
hero_025.teamfight: In teamfights, combine the wind zone and combustion areas to deal continuous magical damage and slow enemies over a wide area. Aim for stuns with Skill 3's hot wind, and use Skill 1's knockback to keep your distance when enemies approach. Fighting within the combustion areas to gain HP recovery and sustain yourself is the key.
hero_025.commonMistakes: A common mistake is not utilizing the wind zone of Skill 1 to expand the combustion areas and relying solely on single-hit damage. Also, be aware that stepping outside the combustion area will cause you to lose the Passive's HP recovery effect, leaving you vulnerable to being killed by enemy attacks.
hero_025.strengths: The ability to generate wide-ranging combustion areas by combining Skill 2 and Skill 1, dealing powerful continuous magical damage, is a major strength. Additionally, it boasts self-protection and disruption capabilities, such as HP recovery within your combustion areas, knockback from Skill 1, and stun from Skill 3.
hero_025.weaknesses: Expanding combustion areas requires coordination with Skill 1; relying on them individually has limited effectiveness. Furthermore, generating the powerful hot wind requires hitting combustion areas with Skill 3, which is a weakness as enemies can easily predict the position and dodge it.

hero_026.earlyGame: Survive the early game by hitting enemy heroes with the main body and puppet's attack animations to collect fragments while recovering HP. Since fragments are also automatically gained every 10-5 seconds, it is crucial to collect 50 fragments to quickly assemble the puppet and prepare to overwhelm enemies with a puppet that lasts for 10 seconds.
hero_026.midGame: Release the puppet with Skill 1 to deal physical damage and knock up enemies along the path, fighting while controlling the puppet. Use Skill 2's four hidden weapons to deal massive damage to enemies on the path, and utilize the purify effect, shield, and movement speed increase gained upon recalling the puppet to balance survival and offense.
hero_026.lateGame: Use the collected fragments to disguise yourself as an enemy hero with the same abilities and launch a powerful ambush. Deal physical damage to enemies with Skill 3's threads; when 2 threads hit, it deals additional damage based on lost HP and slows movement speed to ensure the kill. Utilize the main body's damage reduction and control the puppet carefully.
hero_026.teamfight: Disruption using the puppet is powerful. Use Skill 1's knock up to break the enemy formation, and deal area damage with Skill 2's hidden weapons. Ensure enemies don't escape using the damage and slow from Skill 3's threads. If the puppet is destroyed, the main body gets stunned and cannot summon it for a period, so recalling the puppet before it is destroyed is essential.
hero_026.commonMistakes: A fatal mistake is letting the puppet be automatically destroyed after staying more than 2500 distance away for 5 seconds, or having the enemy destroy it, which strips the main body of vision and stuns it for 2 seconds. Also, reckless charging should be avoided, as the puppet cannot be summoned for 20-16 seconds.
hero_026.strengths: Disruption via the puppet and the Passive ability to transform into enemy heroes are the biggest strengths. It has high attack power with Skill 1's knock up and additional damage and slow from Skill 3's threads. Since recalling the puppet grants a purify effect and shield, it excels in both survival and disruption.
hero_026.weaknesses: The puppet's damage is reduced to 50% of the original, and it takes 100% increased damage, making it fragile. If the puppet is destroyed, the main body is stunned and cannot summon the puppet for a long time, meaning you must always pay close attention to the positioning of the main body and puppet, as well as enemy attacks.

hero_027.earlyGame: Harass enemies by dealing physical damage and slowing them with the shockwave from Skill 1's greatsword, followed by a knock up from the second cast if the first hits. When HP is below 50%, utilize the Passive to recover HP by hitting enemies with skills or normal attacks after taking damage, making it important to fight persistently.
hero_027.midGame: Deal magical damage with Skill 2 while gaining a powerful shield based on max HP to increase durability. Utilize the true damage from the 3 enhanced normal attacks after Skill 2; hitting enemies reduces Skill 3's cooldown, allowing you to create advantageous situations with a balanced offensive and defensive playstyle.
hero_027.lateGame: Initiate into the enemy formation with Skill 3's chain blade by stunning an enemy for 1 second and pulling yourself towards them. Since the pull also deals additional physical damage, combine it with the shield and true damage from enhanced normal attacks to target and eliminate the core enemy hero, gaining the upper hand in combat.
hero_027.teamfight: Initiate fights with the CC-immune dash and stun of Skill 3, and pin down multiple enemies with the knock up from the second stage of Skill 1. Endure enemy attacks with Skill 2's shield, trigger the Passive's HP recovery effect, and steadily chip away at both the enemy frontline and backline with the true damage of enhanced normal attacks.
hero_027.commonMistakes: A mistake is missing the first stage of Skill 1, which prevents the second stage (knock up) from being cast, greatly reducing your disruption capability. Also, Skill 3's chain blade stops at the first enemy hit or at max distance, so hitting an unintended enemy can put you in a perilous situation.
hero_027.strengths: The powerful CC capabilities from Skill 3's stun and pull, and Skill 1's slow and knock up, are its strengths. Combined with high durability from Skill 2's shield and the Passive's HP recovery, as well as the true damage from enhanced normal attacks after Skill 2, it boasts both firepower and durability.
hero_027.weaknesses: Skill 3 stops at the first enemy hit, so being blocked by minions prevents you from approaching the intended target. Also, the Passive's HP recovery effect has a 25-second cooldown, making your durability drop right after it triggers; if enemies outlast the healing, you become much easier to kill.

hero_028.earlyGame: Efficiently deal magical damage and harass enemies by casting Skill 2's water stream to bounce between enemies 5-7 times. Dealing skill damage applies Ice marks; 3 stacks deal additional damage and a 0.75-second freeze effect, so aim at enemy heroes through minions.
hero_028.midGame: Summon an ice pillar with Skill 1 to deal magical damage and a 1-second knock up to enemies in the area, restricting their movement. The ice pillar slows surrounding movement speed, so bouncing Skill 2's water stream off the pillar ensures you stack Ice marks and aim for the freeze effect.
hero_028.lateGame: Form a water zone with Skill 3's water stream, knocking back non-hero units and dealing massive damage and a powerful 90% slow to heroes hit. The water zone lasts 5 seconds, dealing damage and slow every second, completely blocking approaching enemies and creating attack opportunities for your allies.
hero_028.teamfight: Divide the enemy formation with the wide water zone and massive slow from Skill 3. Aim for knock ups with Skill 1's ice pillar while protecting yourself with the invincibility effect when inside the area. Bounce Skill 2's water stream among grouped enemies to continuously freeze multiple targets and dominate the teamfight.
hero_028.commonMistakes: A common failure is not hitting enemy heroes with Skill 3's water stream, preventing the formation of an effective water zone. Also, failing to utilize the invincibility effect from summoning Skill 1's ice pillar and allowing enemies to close in and kill you before you unfreeze yourself prevents you from fully using your disruption abilities.
hero_028.strengths: The ability to apply Ice marks with all skills and the powerful CC from the 3-stack freeze effect are the biggest strengths. The wide-area damage and slow effects from Skill 2's bounces and Skill 3's persistent water zone give her immense control and firepower in chaotic fights where multiple enemies gather.
hero_028.weaknesses: While Skill 3 knocks back non-hero units, the water zone doesn't form until it reaches max distance or hits an enemy hero, creating a massive vulnerability if it misses. Additionally, during Skill 1's invincibility effect, you cannot move, leaving you fragile if you cannot counterattack immediately after it ends.

hero_029.earlyGame: Deal physical damage to enemies on the path with Skill 1's forward slashes, and aim for a knock up on the third slash to gain the upper hand. Casting skills grants enhanced normal attacks; the continuous long-range slashes restore HP, allowing you to sustain in the lane while steadily building up damage.
hero_029.midGame: Slow enemy movement speed by 25% with Skill 2's powerful sword aura, and recover HP by hitting enemy heroes. Hitting with normal attacks reduces Skill 2's cooldown, so combine it with HP recovery from enhanced normal attacks to continuously fire sword auras and gain an advantage in mid-game skirmishes.
hero_029.lateGame: Use Skill 3's blessing power to gain CC immunity, deal damage to surrounding enemies, and massively increase max HP and resistance. During Skill 3, Skill 2 and enhanced normal attacks can fire additional sword auras; use this increased durability and overwhelming attack volume to carve through the enemy formation and dominate late-game fights.
hero_029.teamfight: In teamfights, cast Skill 3 to gain CC immunity and increase your max HP based on the number of nearby enemy heroes, making you a powerful frontline presence. Knock up multiple enemies with the 3rd stage of Skill 1, and maintain the frontline with continuous damage and healing from the additional sword auras and enhanced normal attacks during Skill 3.
hero_029.commonMistakes: Failing to complete all 3 strikes of Skill 1 to miss the knock up, or missing Skill 2 on enemy heroes to lose out on HP recovery are fatal. Also, be careful not to double-tap Skill 3 during its duration, which accidentally cancels the powerful buffs and CC immunity.
hero_029.strengths: Overwhelming durability from Skill 3's CC immunity, max HP increase, and resistance boost is a major strength. It combines HP recovery from enhanced normal attacks after every skill cast with disruption from Skill 1's knock up and Skill 2's slow, boasting incredibly high sustained combat ability in melee range.
hero_029.weaknesses: The healing effect is halved against non-hero units, so you won't get enough sustain fighting minions. Also, Skill 1 must be cast 3 times to trigger the CC, making it hard to utilize if enemies keep their distance, and the drop in durability while Skill 3 is on cooldown is a clear weakness.

hero_030.earlyGame: The Passive allows you to dispel CC with any skill, so you can fight while ignoring enemy disruption. Use Skill 1 to massively boost movement speed, and perform an enhanced normal attack dash to deal physical damage to nearby enemies for ganks or retreats, safely earning EXP and gold in the early game.
hero_030.midGame: Deal area damage and slow with Skill 2's ground stomp, and utilize the buff upon hitting (200% attack speed, 20% lifesteal, HP recovery) to boost your combat power. When hit by CC, dispel it immediately with a skill to gain Fury stacks, strengthening your physical attack and attack range.
hero_030.lateGame: Close in on the target with Skill 3's leaping slash, dealing true damage and a massive slow. For 5 seconds after hitting, all damage dealt inflicts additional true damage equal to 2% of the target's max HP, allowing you to easily destroy even sturdy frontliners with max Fury stacks and overwhelming firepower.
hero_030.teamfight: Dash to the enemy backline using Skill 1's movement speed boost, and deal true damage and slow with Skill 3's leaping slash. Dispel enemy CC with skills to build Fury stacks, and use the extreme attack speed and lifesteal gained from hitting Skill 2 to single-handedly obliterate the enemy formation.
hero_030.commonMistakes: Missing Skill 2 halves the attack speed and lifesteal buffs you gain, making it a fatal mistake to whiff. Also, wasting skills purely to dispel CC, leaving you unable to use Skill 1's dash to engage or Skill 2's buff when truly needed, is a common error.
hero_030.strengths: The ability to dispel CC with all skills, and the Passive that increases physical attack and attack range upon a successful dispel, are the greatest strengths. With additional true damage from Skill 3 and overwhelming attack speed and lifesteal from hitting Skill 2, you unleash unparalleled burst damage and survivability in duels and chaotic fights.
hero_030.weaknesses: A major weakness is the drastic drop in combat power if Skill 2 misses the enemy. Since your strong engage tools are limited to Skill 1's enhanced normal attack or Skill 3, it's hard to catch highly mobile enemies, and once your CC dispel options are exhausted, you are easily killed if focused.

hero_031.earlyGame: Cast skills to build Animosity stacks and gain an advantage with enhanced normal attacks. When enemy HP is above 50%, use the double-sword slash for additional physical damage; when below 50%, use the heavy slash for lost-HP percentage damage. Utilize Skill 2's dash and shield to minimize damage taken while fighting.
hero_031.midGame: Shoot down enemy projectiles with Skill 1's sword aura, dealing physical damage and slow to enemies on the path. Use the super armor during cast to block enemy disruption, rapidly close the gap with a 2-stack Animosity dash, and hit enhanced normal attacks to reduce the CD of Skills 1 and 2, maintaining your offensive momentum.
hero_031.lateGame: Lock onto an enemy hero with Skill 3 and dash to them, dealing area damage and a knock up. This marks the target for a 5-second duel, delaying their healing effects and stripping their survivability. With super armor and 50% damage reduction during the dash, you can perform your role even deep within the enemy formation.
hero_031.teamfight: Shoot down enemy ranged attacks and projectiles with Skill 1, protecting allies while advancing. Lock onto a key enemy target with Skill 3's guaranteed dash, disrupting enemy coordination with the knock up and healing delay. Use enhanced normal attacks from Animosity stacks to reduce skill CDs, and sustain yourself with Skill 2's shield.
hero_031.commonMistakes: A typical mistake is neglecting Animosity stack management and using an enhanced normal attack that doesn't match the enemy's HP situation. Furthermore, if Skill 2 doesn't hit an enemy, you don't get the shield and CD reduction; wasting it for mobility leaves you lacking durability and easily killed in crucial moments.
hero_031.strengths: Unique disruption capabilities like shooting down projectiles and super armor with Skill 1, and the targeted dash and healing delay of Skill 3, are its strengths. Situation-adaptable enhanced normal attacks via Animosity stacks, and high mobility and durability from Skill 2's shield, make it overwhelmingly strong in 1v1s and assassinations.
hero_031.weaknesses: A weakness is that firepower stagnates without consuming Animosity stacks, making the hero highly dependent on skills. Additionally, Skill 3's duel only delays healing effects without fully nullifying them; if enemies heal back up after the effect ends and counterattack, failing to finish them off will leave you at a disadvantage.

hero_032.earlyGame: Hit non-tower, non-crystal enemies with normal attacks to build sword aura stacks, unlocking the Passive to boost physical attack. Harass with physical damage and stuns from the first two dashes of Skill 1, and use the 3rd cast to return to your original position, allowing safe hit-and-run tactics.
hero_032.midGame: Deploy Skill 2's green lotus sword formation; enemies touching the border take physical damage, massive slow, and lose 150 physical defense, boosting your firepower. Use the untargetable state during cast to dodge fatal enemy attacks, quickly stack 4 sword auras, and prepare Skill 3.
hero_032.lateGame: Cast the unlocked Skill 3 with 4 sword aura stacks to turn into a sword aura, unleashing 5 slashes in a designated area. Dive into the enemy formation using the debuff dispel and untargetability upon cast to deal massive area damage, then use the 3rd cast of Skill 1 to safely return to your original position in a classic hit-and-run.
hero_032.teamfight: The basic strategy is to build 4 sword aura stacks on jungle monsters before joining a teamfight. Engage rapidly with Skill 1, use Skill 2's sword formation to lower enemy physical defense while remaining untargetable, and deal wide-area damage with Skill 3's continuous slashes. Then, immediately disengage with Skill 1 to prevent counterattacks.
hero_032.commonMistakes: Plunging into the enemy formation before getting 4 sword aura stacks and dying without casting the powerful Skill 3 is the biggest mistake. Another common failure is when enemies ambush your original position, instantly bursting you down the moment you return with the 3rd cast of Skill 1.
hero_032.strengths: Having two types of untargetable states from Skill 2 and Skill 3, allowing you to completely nullify enemy attacks while dealing one-sided damage, is its greatest strength. The elusive mobility of Skill 1's dashes and return lets you toy with enemies without allowing them a chance to strike back.
hero_032.weaknesses: Overall firepower heavily relies on Skill 3, which requires 4 sword aura stacks, rendering you helpless in areas with no targets to hit with normal attacks. Additionally, Skill 3's damage is reduced by up to 30% when hitting multiple enemy heroes, meaning your damage per target drops when enemies are grouped tightly.

hero_033.earlyGame: Focus on hitting enemies with normal attacks or skills to build stacks of the destruction effect. Fire a burst of 5 bullets in a designated direction with Skill 1, dealing physical damage and recovering energy to harass. Use the 10% movement speed boost to dodge enemy attacks while aiming for the true damage effect at 10 stacks.
hero_033.midGame: Since increasing attack speed adds more bullets to Skill 1, build items to boost your firepower. Once the defense destruction effect triggers on an enemy, normal attacks and skills will deal additional true damage, so use Skill 2's blink to adjust your distance and unleash burst damage.
hero_033.lateGame: Maintain positioning to utilize Skill 2's passive, which grants a 20% damage boost and 15% movement speed increase when enemy heroes are nearby. Cast the bullet-storm of Skill 3 in the middle of the enemy formation, dealing fatal damage to all enemies with true damage from defense destruction and overwhelming fire rate scaling with attack speed.
hero_033.teamfight: Poke from afar with Skill 1 to build destruction stacks, looking for the right moment when defense destruction triggers. Once the enemy's CC skills are on cooldown, close in with Skill 2 and cast Skill 3's barrage to unleash devastating physical and true damage in an area, wiping out the enemy team.
hero_033.commonMistakes: A typical mistake is recklessly jumping into the enemy formation with Skill 3 without having 10 destruction stacks, failing to output true damage and getting killed in return. Also, missing Skill 1 bullets drains your energy, preventing you from using Skill 2's blink when you need it most.
hero_033.strengths: The true damage applied after defense destruction via the Passive is a major strength, allowing you to dish out high damage even against durable tanks. The movement speed boost during Skill 1 and the blink from Skill 2 grant exceptionally high mobility, letting you deal continuous damage from a safe distance.
hero_033.weaknesses: Since you move slowly during Skill 3, taking hard CC (like a stun or knock up) will instantly interrupt the skill and leave you defenseless. Furthermore, to maximize damage, you must build attack speed, which means you often lack damage in the early game before acquiring items.

hero_034.earlyGame: Build up the agility effect through normal attacks to boost attack speed and movement speed, gaining an edge in the lane. Utilize the red (extra magical damage and explosion) and blue (magical damage and slow) tokens fired on every third consecutive attack to clear minions and harass enemy heroes simultaneously.
hero_034.midGame: Fire 6 random tokens in a cone with Skill 1, dealing physical/magical damage and slow effects. Accumulate damage by hitting multiple tokens, and use Skill 2 when enemies approach or CC you; this grants brief invincibility and dispels all debuffs while firing tokens in all directions to counterattack and reposition to safety.
hero_034.lateGame: Fire Skill 3's golden decree at a key enemy target, dealing massive damage and a 1-second stun on hit. It also reduces the target's physical and magical defense by 50% for 5 seconds, allowing you to instantly melt the debuffed enemy using your Passive and Skill 1's barrage, guaranteeing a kill assist for your team.
hero_034.teamfight: Continuously fire tokens with normal attacks and Skill 1 from the backline, maintaining high mobility with the agility effect. Halve the enemy frontline's defense and stun them with Skill 3's decree, then burst them down with the team's combined firepower. When ambushed or hit by CC, quickly disengage using Skill 2's debuff dispel and invincibility.
hero_034.commonMistakes: A major mistake is wasting Skill 2 merely for damage, leaving you unable to dispel debuffs and resulting in death when hit by fatal CC. Also, Skill 3's decree affects the first enemy hit; if it hits a minion or an unintended target, its effectiveness is halved.
hero_034.strengths: Despite being a marksman, the instant debuff dispel and invincibility from Skill 2 provide incredibly high self-preservation, which is a major strength. Additionally, the permanent attack and movement speed boosts from the Passive, combined with the 50% defense reduction from Skill 3, give you the upper hand against any enemy, from tanks to assassins.
hero_034.weaknesses: Missing Skill 3's decree deprives you of your greatest weapon—the defense reduction and stun—drastically lowering your contribution to the team. Also, since you lack mobility skills, even if you dispel debuffs with Skill 2, you cannot cross physical walls, making it difficult to completely escape enemies with persistent pursuit capabilities.

hero_035.earlyGame: Maintain the physical and magical defense boost from the Passive's Mantra Protection triggered by skill casts. Knock up enemies with Skill 1's dash, and deal additional damage and a slow with the enhanced normal attack that follows. Rack up damage and recover HP with Skill 2's rapid punches to stably navigate the early game.
hero_035.midGame: Combine Skill 1's dash and Skill 2's rapid punches to deal damage and sustain. While Skill 2 is active, enhanced normal attacks are automatically thrown, allowing you to stick to enemies and continuously deal max HP percentage damage. Since knocking back an enemy into a wall turns Skill 1 into a flying kick, always be aware of the terrain.
hero_035.lateGame: Knock back enemies with Skill 3's punch; colliding them with a wall deals extra physical damage and a 1-second stun. This transforms Skill 1 into a flying kick that deals extra damage equal to 15% of lost HP, unleashing high assassination potential to one-shot the enemy's main heroes with this combo.
hero_035.teamfight: It's ideal to fight near walls or obstacles, using Skill 3 to smash multiple enemies into a wall simultaneously for wide-area stuns and massive damage. Follow up on stunned enemies with Skill 2's rapid punches and the Passive's enhanced normal attacks, and chase down fleeing enemies with the evolved Skill 1 flying kick to secure the kill.
hero_035.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is failing to smash enemies into a wall with Skill 3, resulting in a mere knockback and missing out on the stun, extra damage, and the transformation of Skill 1 into a flying kick. Furthermore, hitting only non-hero units with Skill 2 and not gaining enough HP recovery is also problematic.
hero_035.strengths: The overwhelming combo damage originating from the powerful stun and extra damage of hitting enemies into a wall with Skill 3, followed by the flying kick, is its strength. High dueling potential from the Passive's defense boost and Skill 2's continuous HP recovery and percentage damage makes him unparalleled when utilizing terrain.
hero_035.weaknesses: Combat effectiveness is highly dependent on the presence of walls; in open areas, Skill 3 cannot show its true value, cutting the threat in half. Also, while you can change directions during Skill 2, your actions are restricted. If you don't cancel it manually, you can't dodge powerful enemy skills, leaving a large opening.

hero_036.earlyGame: Disrupt enemy movements by knocking them back with Skill 1's forward dash and knocking them up with the final slash. When HP drops below 30%, the Passive grants 40% damage reduction; use this durability to play aggressively, dealing guaranteed critical damage with the enhanced normal attack after casting a skill.
hero_036.midGame: Massively reduce enemies' movement speed and attack power with Skill 2's roar, protecting allies while making favorable trades. After casting, the enhanced normal attack sweeps a wide area and recovers HP, allowing you to stay on the frontline. Leverage the trait that increases healing when HP is low to throw off enemies' calculations in edge-of-the-seat battles.
hero_036.lateGame: Deal massive area damage and stun with Skill 3's charged slash. Utilizing the super armor during cast and the "Unyielding" effect that prevents HP from falling below 1, you absorb all focused enemy attacks. Hitting with the blade restores 20% of the damage dealt, making you an unsinkable ship returning to the fray.
hero_036.teamfight: Charge into the enemy formation with Skill 1 to disrupt them with a knockback and knock up, and use Skill 2 to reduce their attack power, lowering damage taken by your whole team. Endure fatal enemy blows with Skill 3's "Unyielding," then stun enemies and recover your HP with a strike that grows stronger the lower your HP is, acting as a solid frontline wall.
hero_036.commonMistakes: The Passive's damage reduction effect only triggers once every 90 seconds, so making reckless charges while it is on cooldown and getting killed is a major mistake. Also, be careful with the direction of Skill 1's dash to avoid pushing an enemy tank into your backline or accidentally helping a fleeing enemy escape.
hero_036.strengths: Absolute invulnerability from Skill 3's "Unyielding" effect and extremely high durability from the Passive's damage reduction are its strengths. Additionally, firepower and healing increase as HP lowers, making you excel in adversity. With abundant CC skills like attack power reduction, knockback, and stun, he is the ideal tank to protect allies.
hero_036.weaknesses: Mobility is limited to Skill 1's dash, which cannot cross walls, making you susceptible to being kited by agile enemies. Furthermore, Skill 3's powerful slash has a charge time, making it easy to dodge, and the extra damage and stun only occur if the tip of the blade hits, requiring very precise positioning.
